incentive.statement: Preserve Andrews authority, typed source and target structure, hostile proof, single-runtime integrity, and browser verification while completing Canvas Lessons 24-25 for ordinary unhyphenated input.

coordinator.responsibility: Coordinate grammar, application, presentation, runtime, and verification until the old explicit-boundary and source-voice gaps are removed.

outcome: Any structurally analyzable Lesson 24-25 source receives the Andrews-licensed causative analyses without requiring editorial hyphens; the selected analysis carries its hidden base and complete source-VNC participant transformation, and the Canvas projection shows both forward and licensed reverse paths without becoming authority.

responsibilities.statement: Own boundary-free morphology, lexical route decisions, hidden nonactive bases, source-voice participant transformations, reverse analyses, and canonical validation.

actors.contribution: Replace explicit-boundary gates and incomplete generic output with typed Canvas Lesson 24-25 analyses.

responsibilities.statement: Normalize source morphology and source voice, then order source analysis, causative formation, participant transform, and any later voice.

actors.contribution: Keep source voice distinct from later target voice and reject caller authority fields.

responsibilities.statement: Expose only genuine source-analysis and causative choices and render canonical forward/reverse Canvas projections.

actors.contribution: Consume the validated application projection without constructing morphology or participant order.

responsibilities.statement: Install every new contract or capability once in the canonical ESM runtime.

actors.contribution: Preserve one runtime lane and direct-import capability parity.

responsibilities.statement: Prove segmented/unsegmented parity, Canvas witnesses and exceptions, hostile authority rejection, source voice, object depth, ambiguity, UI state, and browser behavior.

actors.contribution: Expand the old productive audit into explicit Lesson 24-25 coverage without making fixtures authoritative.

responsibilities.statement: Keep target, board, shared action, rule tags, alignment evidence, and closure state current.

actors.contribution: Keep the approved slice explicit and prevent premature completion claims.

retirements.removalEvidence: The canonical source-analysis frame derives supported internal morphology from lexical identity and typed final shape; segmented and unsegmented inputs share the same route, while user-authored boundaries cannot authorize an option.
The Lesson 24 inventory licenses the Andrews final-i replacement/addition category for Class A and Class B and retains exact witnesses and gaps as more-specific overlays.
Typed Lesson 25 routes now include documented internal alternations, liā/huiā exceptions, hidden Lesson 20 bases, and exact yauh/huāllauh suppletion with its directional target environment.

Source voice is a separate canonical decision before causative formation. Active, passive, and impersonal source machinery transforms through Lesson 23 into one-, two-, and three-object causatives independently of later target voice.

The validated application projection carries the forward derivation and every canonical reverse source analysis, including mach-tia ambiguity; the renderer displays but cannot reconstruct either path.

evidence.result: Entered stems receive typed destockal, root-plus-ya, denominal-ti, exact-alternation, hidden-nonactive, and suppletive analyses without requiring editorial hyphens. Ambiguous internal analyses remain explicit engine-owned choices.

Class A/B final-i replacement and addition, final-a, documented ni/hui preferences, huaqui/yocoya/mahui/quemi alternations, liā/huiā exceptions, and yauh/huāllauh suppletion are represented as Andrews-governed typed routes rather than caller targets or lexical whitelists.

Active, passive, and impersonal source VNCs are selected before causative formation and remain distinct from later target voice. Tests prove one-, two-, and three-object routing, including the Lesson 25.12.3 impersonal two-object to three-object causative.

Caller machinery, source analyses, source nonactive options, reverse analyses, target/formula/surface strings, forged signatures, mutable clones, sparse arrays, and source/later-voice confusion fail closed. Signatures are streamed for latency but every public authorization remains paired with rebuilt exact structural comparison.

The deep-frozen application projection renders the complete source-to-causative formation, participant history, boundary-free analysis, and canonical reverse readings. Presentation validates the enclosing application frame and owns no derivational rule logic.

Focused suites pass source voice 11/11, Lessons 24-26 24/24, application 24/24, Lessons 20-22 30/30, registry 43/43, docs 7/7, and UI 381/381. The canonical ESM suite passes 3393/3393 across 55 suites; Canvas witnesses pass 4/4, all 14 productive families reach depth 3, and Karttunen retains 837 confirmed intersections plus one no-license fixture.

The manifest remains one browser entry with 73/73 modules/installers and alignment is green. Direct-import browser smoke passes with one module script, zero classic scripts, typed authority true, and formula-string authority false. Fresh in-app proof accepts unhyphenated chihua, renders boundary-free and reverse panels, exposes source voice, reveals chihua-lō for impersonal source, and reports no console logs.
